task_completion.visual_evidence: The Microsoft Edge browser window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Edge Extensions management page is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser tab displays the Extensions page, typically with 'edge://extensions' in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Developer mode is enabled in the Extensions page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Developer mode' toggle switch on the Extensions page is turned on.

task_completion.description: The local extension is side-loaded into Edge.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A card or list item for the extension named 'Bug Reporter Internal' is visible on the Extensions page.

task_completion.description: The extension is pinned to the browser toolbar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red extension icon is visible directly on the Edge browser toolbar, typically to the right of the address bar, outside of the extensions dropdown menu.

integrity.description: Verify that Microsoft Edge is the browser being used, not Chrome or Firefox.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Edge-specific UI elements, such as the Edge profile icon, Edge menu styling, or 'edge://' URLs.

integrity.description: Verify the extensions page is genuine and not a mocked local file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The address bar should show 'edge://extensions' and not a 'file://' path or a text editor window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GUI to complete the task rather than just printing success messages in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots must show the actual Edge browser GUI with the extension loaded and pinned, not just a terminal window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The extension to be loaded is named 'Bug Reporter Internal'. It is located at '/home/ga/Documents/BugReporter_v2'. The extension's icon is a solid red square (48x48px). Look for this red icon on the toolbar to verify pinning.
